name: Citation content verification — multi-chunk synthesis

setup.description: Single paper (EMPEROR-Reduced) with 4 chunks covering cardiac results, methods, renal outcomes, and safety data. Tests that the system correctly attributes cardiac data to [1], methods to [2], renal to [3], and safety to [4] without cross-citation.

papers.title: EMPEROR-Reduced: Empagliflozin in Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction

mockChunks.text: Empagliflozin reduced the composite of cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure (HR 0.75; 95% CI 0.65-0.86; P<0.001). The effect was consistent across prespecified subgroups.
A total of 3730 patients with class II-IV heart failure and ejection fraction ≤40% were randomized to empagliflozin 10mg or placebo, with a median follow-up of 16 months.

The annual rate of decline in the estimated glomerular filtration rate was slower in the empagliflozin group than in the placebo group (-0.55 vs -2.28 mL/min/1.73m²/year; P<0.001). Empagliflozin was also associated with a lower risk of serious renal outcomes.
Serious adverse events were reported in 39.3% of patients in the empagliflozin group and 39.1% in the placebo group. Uncomplicated genital tract infections were more common with empagliflozin.

queries.query: What were the main findings of the EMPEROR-Reduced trial, including both cardiac and renal outcomes?
expectedBehavior: Mentions the study population from [2] (3730 patients)
Does NOT cross-cite renal data as [1]
failurePatterns: Renal data cited as [1] instead of [3]

queries.query: Was empagliflozin safe in EMPEROR-Reduced? What were the side effects?
failurePatterns: Fabricates safety data not in chunks

qualityCriteria: No cross-citation errors between different data types
